name: kafka_actions
explanation: Kafka Actions is not a discovery-style metric check. The README states explicitly that the integration is exclusively triggered by Remote Configuration and should never be scheduled manually. The spec marks `remote_config_id` as required and notes 'This ID is automatically set by the Datadog backend via remote configuration. This parameter is REQUIRED — the integration will not start without it.' The check (`KafkaActionsCheck` extending `AgentCheck`) executes one of seven imperative admin actions (read_messages, create_topic, delete_topic, produce_message, etc.) against a user-supplied `kafka_connect_str` bootstrap server, with action-specific payload (cluster ID, topic name, base64-encoded message bytes) supplied by the operator. There is no upstream `kafka_actions` service to probe — the user's intent (which action, which topic, which payload) is the missing input, and the credential/transport fields (SASL, mTLS, OAuth) are user-provided. Pattern N / pattern J apply.
notes: Remote-Configuration-driven action runner, not an autodiscoverable service. Distinct from `kafka` (JMX broker) and `kafka_consumer` (consumer-group offsets).
